title: KING RICHARD THE SECOND
author: William Shakespeare
description: A classic masterpiece by William Shakespeare. Read this Project Gutenberg edition on Bakbak.store.

chapters.title: Introduction
SCENE: Dispersedly in England and Wales.
ACT I
SCENE I. London. A Room in the palace: Enter King Richard, John of Gaunt, with other Nobles and Attendants.
SCENE II. The same. A room in the Duke of Lancaster’s palace: Enter John of Gaunt with the Duchess of Gloucester.
SCENE III. Open Space, near Coventry. Lists set out, and a Throne: Heralds, &c., attending.
SCENE IV. London. A Room in the King’s Castle: Enter King Richard, Green and Bagot at one door; Aumerle at another.
ACT II
SCENE I. London. An Apartment in Ely House: Gaunt on a couch; the Duke of York and Others standing by him.
SCENE II. The Same. A Room in the Castle: Enter Queen, Bushy and Bagot.
SCENE III. The Wolds in Gloucestershire: Enter Bolingbroke and Northumberland with Forces.
SCENE IV. A camp in Wales: Enter Earl of Salisbury and a Welsh Captain.
ACT III
SCENE I. Bristol. Bolingbroke’s camp.
SCENE II. The coast of Wales. A castle in view.
SCENE III. Wales. Before Flint Castle.
SCENE IV. Langley. The Duke of York’s garden: Enter the Queen and two Ladies.
ACT IV
SCENE I. Westminster Hall.
ACT V
SCENE I. London. A street leading to the Tower: Enter the Queen and ladies.
SCENE II. The same. A room in the Duke of York’s palace: Enter York and his Duchess.
SCENE III. Windsor. A room in the Castle: Enter Bolingbroke as King, Harry Percy and other Lords.
SCENE IV. Another room in the Castle: Enter Exton and a Servant.
SCENE V. Pomfret. The dungeon of the Castle: Enter Richard.
SCENE VI. Windsor. An Apartment in the Castle: Flourish. Enter King Henry and York with Lords and Attendants.
